title: tridactyle bicaudata subsp. bicaudata
intro: Tridactyle bicaudata subsp. bicaudata, also known as the Two-Tailed Lipia or Lipia, is a plant species belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Tridactyle bicaudata subsp. bicaudata include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Tridactyle bicaudata subsp. bicaudata is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to be consumed, and its dried leaves can also be used in tinctures and capsules for medicinal purposes.
